text.title: csharp-dotnet Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for csharp-dotnet as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: async void methods outside of event handlers: public async void ProcessAsync() { ... }
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Exceptions thrown inside async void are unobserved — they are posted to the thread pool's unhandled exception handler and crash the process or are silently swallowed, depending on runtime version. Only async Task or async ValueTask are awaitable.

failure-mode.description: Registering a DbContext-holding service as Singleton: builder.Services.AddSingleton<IUserService, UserService>().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DbContext is Scoped by default (one per request). A Singleton that captures a Scoped DbContext holds the same context for the lifetime of the process. EF Core throws InvalidOperationException on the second concurrent request because DbContext is not thread-safe.

failure-mode.description: Missing public partial class Program; at the bottom of Program.cs in projects that have xUnit integration tests using WebApplicationFactory<Program>.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: WebApplicationFactory<Program> requires Program to be a visible type in the application assembly. Without the partial class declaration, the test project cannot reference Program and compilation fails with "Cannot find type Program".

failure-mode.description: Injecting IServiceProvider into a controller or service and calling GetService<T>() inside methods (service locator pattern).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Service locator hides dependencies — they are not visible in the constructor signature, cannot be mocked in unit tests, and are not validated by the DI container at startup. Use constructor injection exclusively.

failure-mode.description: Connection strings or API secrets in appsettings.json committed to source control.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All secrets in appsettings.json are committed to version control and distributed to every developer machine and CI environment. Use dotnet user-secrets locally and Azure Key Vault / environment variables in production.

failure-mode.description: Adding [AllowAnonymous] or overly broad CORS policies as a workaround for authentication errors during development.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: [AllowAnonymous] on a controller disables authorization globally for that controller, including production builds. CORS wildcard (*) allows any origin to make credentialed requests. Both are security regressions that survive code review if not explicitly flagged.
